title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2023-10-20
text: Patient is a 50-year-old female presenting with chest pain radiating to the left arm. History of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Current medications include atorvastatin 40mg and metoprolol 50mg. ECG shows ST elevation. Plan is to admit to CCU and schedule for urgent cardiac catheterization. Vitals: Temp 98.7F, HR 81, BP 158/94, RR 14, SpO2 98% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. Neck: Supple, no JVD or lymphadenopathy. Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1 and S2, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi, except as noted in HPI. Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended, bowel sounds active in all 4 quadrants. Extremities: No clubbing or cyanosis. Pulses 2+ bilaterally. Skin: Warm and dry, no rashes or lesions. Neurological: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor and sensory exams unremarkable. Assessment and Plan: As detailed above. Will follow up in 2 weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.

title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2024-10-10
text: Patient presents with worsening shortness of breath and pitting edema in both lower extremities. Echocardiogram reveals an ejection fraction of 35%. Patient has a history of congestive heart failure. Added furosemide 40mg daily and advised strict fluid restriction. Vitals: Temp 98.5F, HR 93, BP 118/85, RR 21, SpO2 93% on RA. title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2024-06-27
text: A 64-year-old male reports palpitations and dizziness over the last 48 hours. Holter monitor indicates episodes of atrial fibrillation. Patient is currently taking lisinopril 10mg. Plan to start apixaban 5mg twice daily and consult cardiology. Vitals: Temp 98.4F, HR 99, BP 143/83, RR 15, SpO2 99% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2023-07-27
text: Patient evaluated for sharp, pleuritic chest pain. No history of coronary artery disease. Vitals are stable. EKG is unremarkable. Prescribed ibuprofen 600mg for suspected pericarditis. Scheduled for follow-up in one week. Vitals: Temp 98.4F, HR 93, BP 137/77, RR 15, SpO2 97% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress. HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic, PERRLA. title: Cardiac Patient — Visit 2024-03-02
text: Patient is a 78-year-old female with known ischemic cardiomyopathy presenting for routine follow up. Reports stable angina on exertion. Continues to take aspirin 81mg, clopidogrel 75mg. Will order a stress test to evaluate exercise tolerance. Vitals: Temp 98.7F, HR 108, BP 114/88, RR 17, SpO2 99% on RA. General: Alert and oriented x 3, in no acute distress.
